result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
The search result perfectly matches the target paper. Wilson et al. (2017) provides a foundational critique of adaptive gradient methods (Adam, RMSProp, etc.), demonstrating that they often generalize worse than standard SGD despite faster training convergence. This directly addresses a common real-world deep learning pitfall: the blind adoption of popular optimizers without understanding their generalization trade-offs. It fills the missing "optimizers" citation category and will be used to support arguments for principled training methodologies and rigorous validation.
